title: Macrosyringion glutinosum
intro: Macrosyringion glutinosum, also known as Chilean rose or Chilean guayacán, is a plant species native to the Andes in South America, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macrosyringion glutinosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as tinctures and infusions of its leaves and flowers, which are used to treat respiratory issues, colds, and flu.

The exact classification of Macrosyringion glutinosum is not well-documented, but it is often included in the genus Macrosyringion. Macrosyringion glutinosum is closely related to other plants in the Ericaceae family, such as heaths and blueberries.
